id.description: Unique identifier for the College entity.

facilityOverview.description: A brief overview of the college's facilities.

Achievement.title: Achievement
Achievement.description: Represents an academic, research, or extracurricular achievement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Achievement entity.

year.description: The year the achievement was earned.

category.description: The category of the achievement (e.g., academic, research, extracurricular).

CampusImage.title: CampusImage
CampusImage.description: Represents an image of the campus.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CampusImage entity.

category.description: The category of the campus image (e.g., campus areas, labs, library, sports).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

dateTime.description: The date and time of the event.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user (student or faculty) of the portal.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

role.description: The user's role (e.g., student, faculty, admin).

definition.description: Stores college information.  Root level collection.
Stores achievements for a specific college. Includes denormalized 'collegeId' for authorization independence.
Stores campus images for a specific college. Includes denormalized 'collegeId' for authorization independence.

params.name: imageId
params.description: Unique identifier for the campus image.

definition.description: Stores events for a specific college. Includes denormalized 'collegeId' for authorization independence.
Stores user (student and faculty) information for a specific college. Includes denormalized 'collegeId' for authorization independence.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the StudyConnect application, focusing on colleges, their achievements, campus images, events, and associated users (students and faculty). The structure emphasizes Authorization Independence through denormalization and supports secure list operations (QAPs) using structural segregation. A key requirement is supporting a student and faculty login portal.

Colleges are stored in a top-level `colleges` collection. All data associated with a college (achievements, campus images, events, and users) are stored in subcollections under the respective college document. This enforces clear ownership and simplifies security rules.

To achieve Authorization Independence (CRITICAL), the `collegeId` is denormalized into the `achievements`, `campus_images`, `events` and `users` documents. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to the parent `college` document to authorize operations on these subcollections.  Security rules can directly check if `request.resource.data.collegeId == resource.data.collegeId` which enforces that only users or processes acting on behalf of a college can create/modify resources within that college's subcollections. User roles (`student`, `faculty`, `admin`) are stored within the `users` documents for role-based access control.

For QAPs (Rules are Not Filters), each subcollection is homogenous in terms of its security posture. This ensures that listing resources within a subcollection does not require complex filtering logic in the security rules, thus upholding security.
